title: neesiella echioides
intro: Neesiella echioides, also known as the horse's-ear or echinophora, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Neesiella echioides include treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. Neesiella echioides can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or decoction, and is often used in herbal remedies for its therapeutic effects.
